name: SAARC Charter 1985 (South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation)
summary: The Charter of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC Charter) is a regional supranational meta-framework foundational-SAARC-treaty framework adopted by the 7 founding South Asian states in Dhaka — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal South-Asia framework + India-Pakistan-bilateral-tension-paralysed framework (SAARC Charter signed 8 December 1985 in Dhaka Bangladesh by Bangladesh + Bhutan + India + Maldives + Nepal + Pakistan + Sri Lanka + effective 8 December 1985 + Afghanistan admitted 3 April 2007 — formal foundational SAARC treaty framework establishing comprehensive SAARC organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-regional-treaty frameworks which operate as functional regional integration frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal South-Asia framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal India-Pakistan-bilateral-tension-paralysed framework with no SAARC Summit held since 2014 Kathmandu Summit — 11-year paralysis framework as of 2026 + most-populous regional supranational binding-treaty framework with ~1.95 billion population coverage framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal SAARC Convention on Suppression of Terrorism 1987 framework (SAARC Regional Convention on Suppression of Terrorism adopted 4 November 1987 in Kathmandu Nepal + effective 22 August 1988 + Additional Protocol adopted 6 January 2004 in Islamabad Pakistan — formal foundational South-Asia-regional counter-terrorism framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal counter-terrorism framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal SAARC SAFTA South Asian Free Trade Area framework (SAARC Agreement on South Asian Free Trade Area SAFTA signed 6 January 2004 in Islamabad Pakistan + effective 1 January 2006 providing formal South-Asia-regional free-trade framework — formal foundational South-Asia-regional free-trade framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional free-trade framework operating effectively-non-operational due to bilateral-tensions framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal SAARC Convention on Trafficking in Women and Children 2002 framework (SAARC Convention on Preventing and Combating Trafficking in Women and Children for Prostitution signed 5 January 2002 in Kathmandu Nepal + effective 15 November 2005 — formal foundational South-Asia-regional women + children trafficking framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional women + children trafficking framework with PA-intersection framework via cross-border-South-Asian children-trafficking-and-parental-alienation framework). The SAARC Charter is binding upon 8 SAARC member states + 9 SAARC observer states as of 2026 (Afghanistan post-Taliban participation framework uncertain framework + SAARC paralysis framework since 2014) — comprehensive South Asia regional coverage framework. The Charter is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal SAARC Convention on Trafficking in Women and Children 2002 framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal South-Asia-regional cross-border-children's-rights framework. The SAARC Summit + SAARC Council of Ministers + SAARC Standing Committee + SAARC Secretariat are the formal SAARC bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The SAARC Charter is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: SAARC Charter
statutory_framework.relevance: Charter of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation signed 8 December 1985 in Dhaka Bangladesh + effective 8 December 1985 — formal foundational SAARC treaty framework establishing comprehensive SAARC organisation framework.

statutory_framework.citation: SAARC Regional Convention on Suppression of Terrorism 1987
statutory_framework.title: SAARC Convention on Suppression of Terrorism
statutory_framework.relevance: SAARC Regional Convention on Suppression of Terrorism adopted 4 November 1987 in Kathmandu Nepal + effective 22 August 1988 — formal foundational South-Asia-regional counter-terrorism framework.

statutory_framework.citation: SAARC Convention on Trafficking in Women and Children 2002
statutory_framework.title: SAARC Convention on Trafficking in Women and Children
statutory_framework.relevance: SAARC Convention on Preventing and Combating Trafficking in Women and Children for Prostitution signed 5 January 2002 in Kathmandu Nepal + effective 15 November 2005 — formal foundational South-Asia-regional women + children trafficking framework with PA-intersection framework.

statutory_framework.citation: SAARC Agreement on South Asian Free Trade Area SAFTA 2004
statutory_framework.title: SAFTA
statutory_framework.relevance: SAARC Agreement on South Asian Free Trade Area SAFTA signed 6 January 2004 in Islamabad Pakistan + effective 1 January 2006 — formal foundational South-Asia-regional free-trade framework operating effectively-non-operational due to bilateral-tensions framework.

statutory_framework.citation: SAARC Convention on Promotion of Welfare of Children in South Asia 2002
statutory_framework.title: SAARC Convention on Promotion of Welfare of Children
statutory_framework.relevance: SAARC Convention on Regional Arrangements for the Promotion of Child Welfare in South Asia signed 5 January 2002 in Kathmandu Nepal + effective 14 December 2005 — formal foundational South-Asia-regional children's-welfare framework with PA-intersection framework.

apex_courts.name: SAARC Summit (Heads of State or Government)
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for SAARC — paralysed framework with no summit held since 2014.

apex_courts.name: SAARC Council of Ministers
apex_courts.role: Formal foreign ministers body for SAARC.

apex_courts.name: SAARC Standing Committee
apex_courts.role: Formal standing committee body for SAARC.

anonymisation_convention: SAARC-related communications operate via state-state framework; individual communications not formally accepted under SAARC framework.

key_developments.title: Bangladesh proposal for South Asian regional cooperation
key_developments.description: Bangladesh President Ziaur Rahman proposed South Asian regional cooperation framework 2 May 1980 — predecessor framework to SAARC Charter.

key_developments.title: SAARC Charter adopted
key_developments.description: Charter of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation signed 8 December 1985 in Dhaka Bangladesh by Bangladesh + Bhutan + India + Maldives + Nepal + Pakistan + Sri Lanka — formal foundational SAARC treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: SAARC Secretariat established + SAARC Convention on Terrorism
key_developments.description: SAARC Secretariat established 16 January 1987 in Kathmandu Nepal + SAARC Regional Convention on Suppression of Terrorism adopted 4 November 1987 — formal SAARC Secretariat + counter-terrorism framework operational.

key_developments.title: SAARC Convention on Trafficking + SAARC Convention on Child Welfare
key_developments.description: SAARC Convention on Preventing and Combating Trafficking in Women and Children for Prostitution + SAARC Convention on Regional Arrangements for the Promotion of Child Welfare in South Asia signed 5 January 2002 in Kathmandu Nepal — formal foundational South-Asia-regional women + children frameworks adopted.

key_developments.title: SAFTA signed
key_developments.description: SAARC Agreement on South Asian Free Trade Area SAFTA signed 6 January 2004 in Islamabad Pakistan — formal foundational South-Asia-regional free-trade framework adopted.

key_developments.title: SAFTA enters force
key_developments.description: SAARC SAFTA entered force 1 January 2006 — formal South-Asia-regional free-trade framework operational.

key_developments.title: Afghanistan admitted to SAARC
key_developments.description: Afghanistan admitted to SAARC 3 April 2007 at 14th SAARC Summit in New Delhi India — formal SAARC expansion framework.

key_developments.title: 18th SAARC Summit Kathmandu — last summit held
key_developments.description: 18th SAARC Summit held 26-27 November 2014 in Kathmandu Nepal — last SAARC Summit held to date as of 2026.

key_developments.title: 19th SAARC Summit cancelled
key_developments.description: 19th SAARC Summit scheduled for November 2016 in Islamabad Pakistan cancelled following Uri attack 18 September 2016 + India + Bangladesh + Bhutan + Afghanistan boycott — formal SAARC-paralysis framework operational.

structural_findings: SAARC Charter operates a regional supranational binding-treaty framework — places SAARC Charter in the regional supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal South-Asia framework + India-Pakistan-bilateral-tension-paralysed framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal South-Asia framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal India-Pakistan-bilateral-tension-paralysed framework with 11-year paralysis framework as of 2026 + most-populous regional supranational binding-treaty framework with ~1.95 billion population coverage framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal SAARC Convention on Suppression of Terrorism 1987 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal counter-terrorism framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal SAARC SAFTA framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional free-trade framework operating effectively-non-operational due to bilateral-tensions framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal SAARC Convention on Trafficking in Women and Children 2002 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional women + children trafficking framework with PA-intersection framework.

8 SAARC member state + 9 SAARC observer state ratification framework with Afghanistan post-Taliban participation framework uncertain framework + SAARC paralysis framework since 2014 is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive South Asia regional coverage framework.

1980 Bangladesh proposal + 1985 SAARC Charter + 1987 SAARC Secretariat + SAARC Convention on Terrorism + 2002 SAARC Convention on Trafficking + Child Welfare + 2004 SAFTA + 2006 SAFTA effective + 2007 Afghanistan admitted + 2014 last summit + 2016 summit cancelled SAARC-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

SAARC Charter + SAARC Convention on Trafficking in Women and Children + SAARC Convention on Promotion of Welfare of Children + SAFTA + SAARC Convention on Suppression of Terrorism formal quintet framework with formal South-Asia-regional cross-border-children's-rights PA-intersection framework is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: SAARC Charter jurisdiction sidecar — regional supranational South-Asia foundational-SAARC-treaty meta-framework (SAARC Charter 1985 + SAARC Convention on Suppression of Terrorism 1987 + SAARC Convention on Trafficking 2002 + SAARC Convention on Child Welfare 2002 + SAFTA 2004/2006 + Afghanistan 2007 + 1980 Bangladesh proposal predecessor + 2014 last summit + 2016 paralysis). Only modern South Asian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal South-Asia framework globally + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal India-Pakistan-bilateral-tension-paralysed framework with 11-year paralysis as of 2026 + most-populous regional supranational binding-treaty framework with ~1.95 billion population coverage + central jurisdiction of formal SAARC Convention on Suppression of Terrorism 1987 + central jurisdiction of formal SAFTA + central jurisdiction of formal SAARC Convention on Trafficking in Women and Children 2002 + 8 SAARC member states + 9 observer states + 1980 Bangladesh proposal + 1985 SAARC Charter + 1987 SAARC Secretariat + SAARC Convention on Terrorism + 2002 SAARC Convention on Trafficking + Child Welfare + 2004 SAFTA + 2006 SAFTA effective + 2007 Afghanistan admitted + 2014 last summit + 2016 summit cancelled SAARC-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: SAARC Convention on Trafficking in Women and Children 2002 + SAARC Convention on Child Welfare 2002 frameworks operate substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal South-Asia-regional cross-border-children's-rights framework.

Joins regional + supranational + South-Asia-foundational-SAARC-treaty cluster + SAARC-Charter-only-South-Asian-regional-supranational-binding-treaty-globally-distinctive + India-Pakistan-bilateral-tension-paralysed-framework + 11-year-paralysis-since-2014 + most-populous-regional-supranational-binding-treaty-1.95-billion + SAARC-Convention-on-Suppression-of-Terrorism-1987 + SAFTA-2004-2006-effectively-non-operational + SAARC-Convention-on-Trafficking-Women-Children-2002 + SAARC-Convention-on-Child-Welfare-2002 + 8-SAARC-member-states + 9-SAARC-observer-states + Afghanistan-admitted-2007-Taliban-uncertainty + 2014-last-summit-Kathmandu + 2016-Islamabad-summit-cancelled-Uri-attack + South-Asia-regional-cross-border-children-rights-PA-intersection clusters within the corpus.
